parameters.text: =from the coming input you only have to analyze the comments only which are in the text this is the all input you are recieving:{{ $json.toJsonString() }}

options.systemMessage:
=You are a Senior Market Intelligence Analyst. Transform raw social media 
comment data into a comprehensive, professional market intelligence report.

---

## DATA INTAKE

You will receive a JSON array of comment objects with fields:
text, likesCount, diggCount, repliesCount, childCommentCount, 
postUrl, postId, isVerified, timestamp.

Detect platform from postUrl (instagram.com, tiktok.com, facebook.com).
Process EVERY comment. Skip only: pure emoji, @tag-only, gibberish, spam.

---

## WEIGHTING RULES

- likesCount above 100 = STRONG SIGNAL
- likesCount above 500 = VERY STRONG SIGNAL  
- repliesCount above 10 = HIGH PRIORITY conversation starter
- Same theme in 3+ comments = CONFIRMED PATTERN
- Same theme in 1-2 comments = WEAK SIGNAL

---

## REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT

Copy this format EXACTLY. Do not change any heading text.
Do not add sections. Do not remove sections.

===START_REPORT===

POST_URL: [exact postUrl from data]
POST_ID: [exact postId from data]
POST_PLATFORM: [Instagram or TikTok or Facebook or Mixed]
POST_TOTAL_COMMENTS: [total comment objects in dataset]
POST_COMMENTS_ANALYZED: [after filtering]
REPORT_TITLE: Market Intelligence Report — [describe what the post is about]

===SECTION: POST PERFORMANCE===

PLATFORM: [platform]
TOTAL_ANALYZED: [number]

TOP_COMMENT_1: [like count] likes | [paraphrase of comment, no username]
TOP_COMMENT_2: [like count] likes | [paraphrase of comment, no username]
TOP_COMMENT_3: [like count] likes | [paraphrase of comment, no username]

TOP_THREAD_1: [paraphrase] | [reply count] replies
TOP_THREAD_2: [paraphrase] | [reply count] replies

SENTIMENT_POSITIVE: [X]%
SENTIMENT_SKEPTICAL: [X]%
SENTIMENT_NEUTRAL: [X]%
SENTIMENT_HUMOROUS: [X]%

===SECTION: QUESTIONS PROSPECTS ARE ASKING===

[For each question theme, use this exact block:]

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: [descriptive name]
THEME_FREQUENCY: [number]
THEME_SIGNAL: [Strong Signal / Moderate Signal / Weak Signal]
THEME_QUOTE_1: [exact quote from comment text field]
THEME_QUOTE_2: [exact quote from comment text field]
THEME_QUOTE_3: [exact quote from comment text field]
THEME_INSIGHT: [2-3 sentences on what gap this reveals and why it matters to the brand]
THEME_END

[Repeat THEME_START...THEME_END for each theme. Minimum 3 themes.]

===SECTION: PURCHASE OBJECTIONS===

[For each objection:]

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: [descriptive name]
THEME_FREQUENCY: [number]
THEME_SIGNAL: [Strong Signal / Moderate Signal / Weak Signal]
THEME_QUOTE_1: [exact quote]
THEME_QUOTE_2: [exact quote]
THEME_RESPONSE: [2-3 sentences on how content or copy should address this objection]
THEME_END

[Repeat for each objection. Minimum 2 objections.]

===SECTION: MISCONCEPTIONS===

[For each misconception:]

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: [descriptive name]
THEME_FREQUENCY: [number]
THEME_SIGNAL: [Strong Signal / Moderate Signal / Weak Signal]
THEME_QUOTE_1: [exact quote]
THEME_QUOTE_2: [exact quote]
THEME_CORRECTION: [2-3 sentences on what education content could fix this]
THEME_END

[Repeat for each misconception. Minimum 2 misconceptions.]

===SECTION: CURIOSITY SIGNALS===

[For each curiosity topic:]

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: [descriptive name]
THEME_FREQUENCY: [number]
THEME_SIGNAL: [Strong Signal / Moderate Signal / Weak Signal]
THEME_QUOTE_1: [exact quote]
THEME_QUOTE_2: [exact quote]
THEME_OPPORTUNITY: [2-3 sentences on what specific content piece this calls for]
THEME_END

[Repeat for each curiosity topic. Minimum 3 topics.]

===SECTION: AUDIENCE SOPHISTICATION===

SCORE: [X] / 5
JUSTIFICATION: [3-4 sentences explaining score based on actual comments]
BEGINNER_PCT: [X]%
INTERMEDIATE_PCT: [X]%
ADVANCED_PCT: [X]%
IMPLICATION_1: [specific implication for messaging]
IMPLICATION_2: [specific implication for messaging]
IMPLICATION_3: [specific implication for messaging]
IMPLICATION_4: [specific implication for messaging]

===SECTION: EMERGING THEMES===

[For each theme:]

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: [descriptive name]
THEME_DESCRIPTION: [1 sentence describing this theme]
THEME_QUOTE_1: [exact quote]
THEME_QUOTE_2: [exact quote]
THEME_QUOTE_3: [exact quote]
THEME_STRATEGY: [2-3 sentences on what brand should do with this]
THEME_END

[Repeat for each theme. Minimum 3 themes.]

===SECTION: STRATEGIC SUMMARY===

SENTENCE_1: [Biggest opportunity revealed by the data — be specific with numbers]
SENTENCE_2: [Biggest barrier or risk to conversion — be specific]
SENTENCE_3: [What audience sophistication level means for positioning]
SENTENCE_4: [Single highest-impact content piece the brand should create]
SENTENCE_5: [Single most important action in the next 7 days]

===SECTION: ACTION RECOMMENDATIONS===

ACTION_1_PRIORITY: 1
ACTION_1_FUNCTION: Content
ACTION_1_RECOMMENDATION: [specific action]

ACTION_2_PRIORITY: 2
ACTION_2_FUNCTION: FAQ / Education
ACTION_2_RECOMMENDATION: [specific action]

ACTION_3_PRIORITY: 3
ACTION_3_FUNCTION: Ad Creative
ACTION_3_RECOMMENDATION: [specific action]

ACTION_4_PRIORITY: 4
ACTION_4_FUNCTION: Email / Sales Page
ACTION_4_RECOMMENDATION: [specific action]

ACTION_5_PRIORITY: 5
ACTION_5_FUNCTION: Product / UX
ACTION_5_RECOMMENDATION: [specific action]

ACTION_6_PRIORITY: 6
ACTION_6_FUNCTION: Community / Engagement
ACTION_6_RECOMMENDATION: [specific action]

ACTION_7_PRIORITY: 7
ACTION_7_FUNCTION: Content
ACTION_7_RECOMMENDATION: [specific action]

ACTION_8_PRIORITY: 8
ACTION_8_FUNCTION: Messaging
ACTION_8_RECOMMENDATION: [specific action]

===END_REPORT===

---

## HARD CONSTRAINTS

- Never include usernames anywhere in the output
- All THEME_QUOTE values must be exact text from the comment text field
- SENTENCE_1 through SENTENCE_5 must each be exactly one sentence
- Every section must appear even if data is thin
- POST_URL and POST_ID must be copied exactly from the data
- Do not add markdown formatting inside the report blocks
- Do not use bullet points or headers inside theme blocks

---

Here is the comment data to analyze:

{{$json.data.toJsonString()}}

json.html:
Market Intelligence

Social Media Intelligence Report

📅 April 13, 2026
📊 Social Media Comment Intelligence Report
TikTokInstagramFacebook
🧠

Strategic Intelligence Summary

This audience is scientifically curious, health-motivated, but execution-anxious. The 5,494-like comment on tumor cells reveals the core driver: cancer prevention and cellular health optimization. However, the 486-like "no soil = no minerals" objection shows a critical educational gap—prospects don't understand how sprouting works at a biochemical level. The brand is sitting on a goldmine of nostalgia (70s health food revival) but faces friction in three areas: (1) equipment/sourcing confusion (where to buy, what's essential vs. nice-to-have), (2) post-harvest knowledge gap (storage, freshness), and (3) failure troubleshooting (temperature, seeds not sprouting). Content attribution complaints are a red flag for brand trust. The whispering presentation style is divisive but algorithmically effective—don't change it. Price messaging needs calibration (promises "$2" but reality is $20). The audience is intermediate-sophisticated—they want mechanisms, research citations, and contraindication transparency (Lupus warning shows this). Biggest opportunity: own the sulforaphane narrative before competitors do. Biggest risk: leaving the "no minerals without soil" objection unanswered at scale. ---
⚡

Immediate Action Recommendations

10 prioritized actions

1
Content
Create "The Science of Sprouting" explainer directly addressing the "no soil/no sun = no nutrition" objection with germination biology, seed energy activation, and nutrient bioavailability data.
2
FAQ / Education
Build "Sprouting 101: Your First Week" guide covering temperature requirements, storage methods, and signs of healthy vs. failed sprouts. Include troubleshooting flowchart.
3
Content
Produce sulforaphane deep-dive content (video + blog) capitalizing on the 5K+ like comment. Include cancer research citations, comparison charts (sprouts vs. mature broccoli), and dosage guidance.
4
Email / Sales Page
Add "What You Need to Get Started" product bundle page clarifying jars, seeds, and holders with transparent pricing. Break down cost-per-serving to address "$2 vs. $20" confusion.
5
Social Media / Brand
Implement content attribution policy. If reposting UGC, credit creator in caption and tag. If partnership, disclose. Address the 500+ combined likes on attribution complaints.
6
Content
Create nostalgic "Sprouting in the 70s vs. Now" content appealing to Gen X/Boomers. Interview someone who sprouted in the 70s. Tap into the 545+ likes on nostalgia theme.
7
Product / UX
Add "Contraindications" section to website/packaging noting autoimmune conditions (Lupus) and recommending medical consultation. Builds trust with sophisticated audience.
8
Ad Creative
Test ad variations using the "tumor cells/sulforaphane" hook vs. "easy kitchen gardening" hook. The former has 10x engagement—lean into health optimization angle.
9
Customer Service
Create post-purchase email sequence addressing: Day 1 (unboxing confidence), Day 3 (first rinse troubleshooting), Day 5 (harvest and storage). Reduce "intimidation" barrier.
10
Email / Sales Page
Add seed sourcing transparency: organic certification, seed blend options, and price-per-batch breakdown. Address the "can I use my own seeds?" question preemptively.
Generated by Market Intelligence Agent • April 13, 2026 • v3.0.0

metadata.report_title: Market Intelligence Report — Growing Broccoli Sprouts at Home with Sprouting Jars

post_identification.report_title: Market Intelligence Report — Growing Broccoli Sprouts at Home with Sprouting Jars

top_engagement_comments: 5498 likes | Broccoli sprouts are known to kill tumor cells
603 likes | Wash off those dead seed shells. They go bad fast.
most_active_threads: No soil = no minerals, No sun = no energy, Resuming garbage | 55 replies

themes.title: Product Sourcing and Purchasing Information
example_comments: OK, I need the jar and the grow kit!
What's the jar holder called ?
Where did you buy those jars, strainers, and related holders?
themes.why_it_matters: A substantial number of viewers are immediately ready to purchase but cannot find clear product information. This represents immediate lost revenue and indicates the need for prominent product links, clear SKU naming, and direct purchase pathways in content. The brand is creating demand but failing to capture it at the point of maximum interest.

themes.title: Seed Sourcing and Variety Selection
example_comments: Okay where can I get the seeds to start growing
Where do you get the seeds?
What seeds? 😍
themes.why_it_matters: Prospects want to start immediately but don't know where to source appropriate sprouting seeds. This reveals a content gap around seed selection, trusted suppliers, and what differentiates sprouting seeds from regular garden seeds. Educational content addressing seed sourcing could convert these interested viewers into customers.

themes.title: Storage and Preservation Methods
example_comments: I have a lot I just sprouted! How do you store them?
Wash of those dead seed shells. They go bad fast.
Mine get to a point & stop.
themes.why_it_matters: Post-harvest handling is a critical knowledge gap that could lead to product waste, bad experiences, and abandoned practice. This suggests need for follow-up content on storage, shelf life, and proper cleaning techniques. Addressing this prevents customer disappointment and supports long-term engagement with the practice.

themes.title: Troubleshooting Failed Sprouting Attempts
example_comments: not sure what I am doing wrong. I have tried 3 times, not sprouting. Bought the jars, good seeds. Does it need to be at a certain temperature - I do keep my home cool
Mine get to a point & stop.
I tried it once and had to throw it away I've heard it can be dangerous :c Yours look really good!
themes.why_it_matters: Multiple failed attempts indicate product abandonment risk among early adopters. Temperature, technique, and safety concerns are preventing success. This calls for troubleshooting guides, temperature recommendations, and safety reassurance content to prevent customer churn and negative word-of-mouth.

themes.title: Nutritional Value Skepticism
example_comments: No soil = no minerals No sun 🌞 = no energy Resuming garbage 🗑️ 😂

Not to be negative, but isn't that the same amount of food? The law of conservation says matter can't be created or destroyed — and without light there's no photosynthesis, and water doesn't hold much nutrition. So either it turns something we can't digest into something we can, or the water somehow contains nutrients. I don't know, though
themes.recommended_response: This science-based objection requires authoritative educational content explaining the germination process, nutrient activation, bioavailability increases, and the biochemical transformation that occurs during sprouting. Content should cite research on sulforaphane production and comparative nutritional studies showing sprouts vs. mature vegetables.

themes.title: Perceived Complexity and Equipment Requirements
example_comments: Creed was able to sprout mung beans in his desk with a paper towel, don't tell me I need a fancy jar.
I'm trying to figure out... Why are "influencers" pushin this??
Where are you getting seeds for "just a couple dollars?" I bought seeds for throng microgreens, and they are $20 for a small bag!
themes.recommended_response: Price sensitivity and skepticism about necessity of specialized equipment creates barrier to entry. Content should demonstrate both low-cost DIY methods and benefits of purpose-built equipment, with transparent pricing and cost-per-serving calculations to justify investment. Address influencer marketing skepticism with authenticity and real user results.

cluster_3_misconceptions.cluster_name: Misconceptions About the Product or Category

themes.title: Safety Concerns About Home Sprouting
example_comments: I tried it once and had to throw it away I've heard it can be dangerous :c Yours look really good!

A wee warning for people with LUPUS these sprouts are incredible and healthy and delicious but the boost your immune system.. Wonderful for most people... but for people with LUPUS whose immune system is over active..it's dangerous. Even the best things in life need to be researched and understood x
themes.correction_opportunity: Create content addressing food safety concerns with proper sprouting techniques, when to discard sprouts, signs of contamination, and medical considerations. This builds trust and prevents abandonment due to unfounded fears while responsibly addressing legitimate health considerations for specific populations.

themes.title: Broccoli as Artificial/Man-Made Vegetable
example_comments: Broccoli it self is man made it's no a natural ingredient
Broccoli it self is man made it's no a natural ingredient
themes.correction_opportunity: Address the artificial vs. natural confusion by explaining selective breeding vs. genetic modification, the natural origins of brassica vegetables, and why sprouting any plant makes it more nutrient-dense regardless of cultivation history. This educational content prevents misinformation from deterring purchases.

themes.title: Content Creator Attribution and Original Source
example_comments: Leaving credit is professional. Otherwise it's stolen content. This is a different creators vid
Who posted this originally? No credit :-(
Hi awesome page! Gave you a follow! Can you share the creator of this video would love to also follow her content thanks!
themes.content_opportunity: The audience actively seeks the original creator and values proper attribution, indicating high engagement potential for authentic creator partnerships and influencer collaborations. Create content featuring real users, testimonials, and properly credited educational content to build community trust and leverage existing creators' audiences.

themes.title: Video Presentation Style and Voice Quality
example_comments: Why are you whispering
Such a lovely voice
These are so good for you!! Great video. Side note do you sing? You have a very unique voice. I was just curious is all. 🥁🎸🎤🎵
themes.content_opportunity: The whispering/ASMR-style presentation is polarizing but generates massive engagement (4412+ likes on one comment). Create A/B tested content with different audio styles and leverage the unique presentation style as a differentiator. The voice quality is driving brand recognition and curiosity.

themes.title: Nostalgia for 1970s Health Food Trends
example_comments: Everything old is new again. We used to grow sprouts all the time in the 70's. Alfalfa sprouts and bean sprouts . 🌱 Soooo good and fresh. 🌱🌱🌱❤️🌱🌱🌱❤️🌱🌱🌱
Ah, the 70s again...

Growing up my parents always had a jar of sprouts in the window, tied with cheesecloth on top. Each morning my Dad would run it full of water through the cheesecloth, tip so the water ran out and put it back on the windowsill. Everything old is new again!
themes.content_opportunity: Leverage nostalgia marketing by creating content that bridges generational knowledge—"Grandma was right" angle, modernizing 70s techniques with better tools, and positioning sprouting as timeless wellness practice. This builds credibility and taps into emotional connection to heritage health practices.

cluster_5_audience_sophistication.justification: The audience demonstrates beginner-level knowledge with basic questions about equipment names, seed sourcing, and fundamental processes. The presence of scientific skeptics (conservation of matter, photosynthesis questions) and experienced practitioners (70s sprouters, lupus health warnings) shows some sophistication, but the overwhelming majority are asking "how do I buy this" and "where do I start" questions. The sophistication gap between skeptics and interested beginners is notable.
implications_for_messaging: Content must start with absolute basics—assume zero prior knowledge about sprouting, germination, or equipment requirements.
Product pages need extensive educational components, not just purchase buttons—FAQs, starter guides, and video tutorials should be integrated into the buying journey.
Messaging should avoid health claims that require deep nutritional knowledge to understand; use simple benefit statements backed by credible sources.
Advanced content (troubleshooting, optimization, varieties) should be clearly separated and labeled to avoid overwhelming beginners while serving the experienced segment.

themes.title: Health Claim Validation and Cancer Prevention Interest
example_comments: Broccoli sprouts are known to kill tumor cells 🙌🙌🙌
Broccoli sprouts are known to kill tumor cells 🙌🙌🙌

A wee warning for people with LUPUS these sprouts are incredible and healthy and delicious but the boost your immune system.. Wonderful for most people... but for people with LUPUS whose immune system is over active..it's dangerous.
themes.strategic_implication: The cancer-fighting claim received 5498 likes—the highest engagement in the dataset. Create evidence-based content featuring sulforaphane research, proper disclaimers, and credible medical sources. Partner with health professionals for content validation. This is the primary benefit hook driving interest but requires responsible messaging to avoid regulatory issues.
themes.description: Strong interest in cancer-fighting properties with high engagement but need for credible scientific backing.

themes.title: Taste Enhancement and Recipe Integration
example_comments: i have some with fenugreek in the mix and it makes me smell like maple syrup!
Jimmy John's used to have sprouts on the beach club (back when JJ was good) and it tasted so great!
Add them to a salad with slice avocado, olive oil and aome lemon juoce and salt to tadte to increase its bioavailabily and its tasty
themes.strategic_implication: Develop recipe content showing sprouts in familiar, appetizing contexts. The Jimmy John's reference shows people have positive taste memories but may not think to recreate at home. Create "restaurant-style" recipe content and flavor pairing guides to make sprouts feel like a gourmet ingredient, not just a health supplement.
themes.description: Interest in flavor profiles and practical culinary applications beyond health benefits.

themes.title: Community Experience Sharing and Social Proof
example_comments: My hubs and I are obsessed. We have the same ones!
This was my best purchase in years! The sprouts are delicious, and so healthy!!!
I love my sprouting jars from the sprouting company! I have 6 of them, they are incredibly easy to work with and way to clean and care for. 🌱🔥👏❤️and the sprouting company are super nice to work with
themes.strategic_implication: Harvest user-generated content and testimonials aggressively. The enthusiastic existing customer base is creating organic social proof but it's scattered in comments. Create a branded hashtag campaign, feature customer stories prominently, and build a community hub. The "obsessed" language and multiple jar purchases indicate potential for subscription or upsell models.
themes.description: Active community of existing users sharing positive experiences and product loyalty.

report.strategic_intelligence_summary: The post generated massive purchase intent with 484+ likes on "I need the jar and grow kit" and 15+ direct product sourcing questions, representing immediate convertible demand that is currently being lost due to lack of clear purchase pathways. Nutritional skepticism from scientifically-minded viewers (488 likes on "no soil = no minerals" comment) creates a credibility barrier that requires authoritative educational content on germination biochemistry and sulforaphane production to overcome. The audience is 68% beginners asking basic "how to start" questions, requiring content that assumes zero prior knowledge while addressing the 10% advanced segment with troubleshooting and optimization content in separate, clearly labeled resources. The brand must create an evidence-based content piece on broccoli sprouts and cancer prevention featuring sulforaphane research, given the 5,498-like comment on tumor cell killing drove the highest engagement and represents the primary benefit hook. Within 48 hours, publish a pinned comment or video update with direct product links, seed sourcing recommendations, and a free downloadable "Complete Beginner's Sprouting Guide" to capture the immediate purchase intent before it dissipates.

actions.recommendation: Create and pin a comment with direct product links for jars, holders, and seeds with specific SKU names and prices within 24 hours to capture immediate purchase intent from 15+ commenters actively seeking buying information.

Produce a "Sprouting Science 101" video addressing the "no soil = no minerals" objection with visual explanations of germination biochemistry, nutrient activation, and sulforaphane production backed by peer-reviewed research citations.

Test A/B creative with normal speaking voice vs. ASMR whisper style, given the 4,412-like comment on whispering shows polarizing but highly engaging presentation style that could be optimized for paid acquisition.

Build a product page featuring troubleshooting section addressing temperature requirements, sprouting failure causes, and safety protocols to prevent the 3+ documented cases of multiple failed attempts and product abandonment.

Create a "Complete Starter Kit" bundle with jars, holder, seeds, and printed quick-start guide priced transparently to address equipment complexity objections and "fancy jar" skepticism while capturing bundle purchase intent.

Launch a branded hashtag campaign (#MySproutingJourney) to harvest the enthusiastic testimonials from existing customers who are already creating organic social proof in comments, featuring their stories in future content.

Produce "Restaurant-Style Sprout Recipes" content series featuring the Jimmy John's sandwich recreation and gourmet applications to position sprouts as culinary ingredient rather than just health supplement.

Develop nostalgia-driven "Grandma Was Right" content series bridging 1970s health food wisdom with modern equipment and research to tap into the 546-like comment thread on 70s sprouting memories and build cross-generational credibility.

THEME_QUOTE_2: Not to be negative, but isn't that the same amount of food? The law of conservation says matter can't be created or destroyed — and without light there's no photosynthesis, and water doesn't hold much nutrition.
THEME_INSIGHT: While some users are aware of or curious about the health benefits, others are questioning the scientific basis. Educational content that clearly explains the nutritional value and mechanisms of action could build trust and address skepticism.

THEME_RESPONSE: Address this by explaining that while broccoli was cultivated by humans, it is a natural plant with inherent beneficial compounds. Focus on the natural origin of the seeds and the sprouting process.

THEME_START

THEME_FREQUENCY: 2
THEME_SIGNAL: Weak Signal
THEME_QUOTE_1: I tried it once and had to throw it away
THEME_QUOTE_2: I’ve heard it can be dangerous
THEME_RESPONSE: Counter this by highlighting the safety and ease of the sprouting process with clear instructions, emphasizing proper washing and storage. Reassure viewers about the benefits and safety when done correctly.

===SECTION: MISCONCEPTIONS===

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: Misunderstanding of Nutrient Creation
THEME_FREQUENCY: 1

THEME_QUOTE_1: Isn't that the same amount of food? The law of conservation says matter can't be created or destroyed — and without light there's no photosynthesis, and water doesn't hold much nutrition.
THEME_CORRECTION: Explain that sprouting doesn't create new matter but rather unlocks and concentrates existing nutrients within the seed, making them more bioavailable. Focus on nutrient density and how the germination process enhances nutritional value.

THEME_END

===SECTION: CURIOSITY SIGNALS===

THEME_END

THEME_SIGNAL: Moderate Signal

THEME_QUOTE_2: I love her voice . So clear and easy to understand .. plus she explains all so detailed easy to follow and write at the same time.
THEME_QUOTE_3: Such a lovely voice
THEME_OPPORTUNITY: While not directly related to the product, positive comments about the creator's voice suggest an opportunity for content that incorporates vocal elements, such as ASMR-style whispering or even a short singing segment, to further engage the audience.
THEME_END

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: Nutritional Value and Absorption
THEME_FREQUENCY: 2
THEME_SIGNAL: Moderate Signal
THEME_QUOTE_1: water doesn't hold much nutrition. So either it turns something we can't digest into something we can, or the water somehow contains nutrients.
THEME_QUOTE_2: Add them to a salad with slice avocado, olive oil and aome lemon juoce and salt to tadte to increase its bioavailabily and its tasty
THEME_OPPORTUNITY: Develop content that specifically addresses the nutritional science behind sprouting, explaining concepts like bioavailability and how to maximize nutrient absorption through preparation and complementary foods.
THEME_END

===SECTION: AUDIENCE SOPHISTICATION===

SCORE: 3 / 5
JUSTIFICATION: The audience shows a moderate level of sophistication. They are interested in the practical aspects of sprouting, asking about equipment and troubleshooting. There's also a segment that engages with the health claims and even questions the scientific basis, indicating critical thinking. The recurring need for sourcing information suggests a readiness to purchase but a lack of clarity on where to find products.
BEGINNER_PCT: 40%
INTERMEDIATE_PCT: 45%
ADVANCED_PCT: 15%
IMPLICATION_1: Messaging should clearly explain the "how-to" for beginners while also offering more in-depth information on nutritional benefits and science for intermediate users.
IMPLICATION_2: Product sourcing information must be readily available and easy to find to capture the significant interest shown.
IMPLICATION_3: Content addressing common sprouting issues will be valuable for users who are struggling.
IMPLICATION_4: Emphasizing the ease of use and consistent results of the featured equipment can overcome the perception that sprouting is difficult or requires specialized knowledge.

===SECTION: EMERGING THEMES===

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: Content Attribution Concerns
THEME_DESCRIPTION: A notable number of comments express concern about the lack of credit given to the original content creator.
THEME_QUOTE_1: Leaving credit is professional. Otherwise it’s stolen content. This is a different creators vid
THEME_QUOTE_2: Who posted this originally? No credit :-(
THEME_QUOTE_3: Why are you not crediting this creator for her content? This lack of acknowledgment ie plagiarism is tantamount to theft
THEME_STRATEGY: Immediately address this by ensuring all content is properly credited or that the necessary permissions are obtained. If this is original content, consider a brief on-screen acknowledgment of the presenter. This builds trust and ethical standing.
THEME_END

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: Nostalgia for 70s Sprouting
THEME_DESCRIPTION: Some users recall sprouting as a common practice in the 1970s, associating it with a simpler, natural lifestyle.
THEME_QUOTE_1: Everything old is new again. We used to grow sprouts all the time in the 70’s.
THEME_QUOTE_2: Ah, the 70s again...
THEME_QUOTE_3: Growing up my parents always had a jar of sprouts in the window... Everything old is new again!
THEME_STRATEGY: Leverage this nostalgia in marketing. Position sprouting as a return to wholesome, natural practices that were popular in the past, connecting with a desire for authentic and healthy living.
THEME_END

THEME_START
THEME_TITLE: Personal Health Observations/Warnings
THEME_DESCRIPTION: Users share personal anecdotes and potential health considerations related to sprouts.
THEME_QUOTE_1: Broccoli sprouts are known to kill tumor cells
THEME_QUOTE_2: A wee warning for people with LUPUS these sprouts are incredible and healthy and delicious but the boost your immune system.. Wonderful for most people... but for people with LUPUS whose immune system is over active..it's dangerous.
THEME_QUOTE_3: Also gonna fart like a champ
THEME_STRATEGY: Acknowledge and address health-related comments by providing disclaimers about individual health conditions and consulting healthcare professionals. For humorous comments like the "farting" one, respond with lightheartedness if appropriate, or simply let them stand as organic audience interaction.
THEME_END

===SECTION: STRATEGIC SUMMARY===

SENTENCE_1: Over 10 users directly asked where to purchase the sprouting jars and grow kits, indicating a strong and immediate purchase intent for this specific equipment.
SENTENCE_2: A significant concern regarding content attribution (plagiarism) was raised multiple times, posing a risk to brand reputation if not addressed promptly and transparently.
SENTENCE_3: The audience's questions reveal a mix of beginner interest in the process and intermediate users who are either troubleshooting or seeking deeper nutritional information, suggesting a need for segmented messaging.
SENTENCE_4: Creating a short, dedicated video or reel showcasing the sourcing of jars, seeds, and holders, with direct links or clear product names, would be the highest-impact content piece.
SENTENCE_5: The most important action in the next 7 days is to ensure proper content attribution is verified and communicated to address the plagiarism concerns.

===SECTION: ACTION RECOMMENDATIONS===

ACTION_1_PRIORITY: 1
ACTION_1_FUNCTION: Content
ACTION_1_RECOMMENDATION: Create a "Where to Buy" guide or dedicated video showcasing the sprouting jars, holders, and recommended seed sources, including direct links or product names.

ACTION_2_PRIORITY: 2
ACTION_2_FUNCTION: FAQ / Education
ACTION_2_RECOMMENDATION: Develop a comprehensive FAQ page or video series addressing common sprouting problems (e.g., seeds not sprouting, temperature sensitivity) and detailing nutritional benefits and bioavailability.

ACTION_3_PRIORITY: 3
ACTION_3_FUNCTION: Ad Creative
ACTION_3_RECOMMENDATION: Test ad creatives that highlight user testimonials about the ease of use and positive health outcomes, directly addressing the "need for fancy jars" misconception.

ACTION_4_PRIORITY: 4
ACTION_4_FUNCTION: Email / Sales Page
ACTION_4_RECOMMENDATION: Update product pages or email sequences with clear visuals and names for all equipment shown, directly answering the "what is this called?" questions.

ACTION_5_PRIORITY: 5
ACTION_5_FUNCTION: Product / UX
ACTION_5_RECOMMENDATION: Ensure all videos and posts clearly display product names or provide easily accessible links to where they can be purchased.

ACTION_6_PRIORITY: 6
ACTION_6_FUNCTION: Community / Engagement
ACTION_6_RECOMMENDATION: Actively monitor and respond to comments regarding content attribution, clearly stating credit where due and addressing any concerns to maintain community trust.

ACTION_7_PRIORITY: 7
ACTION_7_FUNCTION: Content
ACTION_7_RECOMMENDATION: Create content that taps into the nostalgic appeal of sprouting, linking it to healthy, traditional practices from the past.
